title: Numbers & Time: Making Plans Clearly
objectives: Use numbers, clock time, days, and calendar phrases to make simple plans with Carlos.
Understand Carlos in short guided exchanges.
Use the lesson language in one realistic conversation.
objective: Use numbers, clock time, days, and calendar phrases to make simple plans with Carlos.

grammar.topic: Telling time with es la / son las
grammar.explanation: Use es la una for one o?clock and son las for every other hour. Use a las to say when something happens.
grammar.practicePrompt: Say one original sentence using today?s pattern with Carlos.

culture.title: Numbers & Time in Real Life
culture.text: In many Spanish-speaking places, people use both 12-hour and 24-hour time. For casual plans, context usually makes morning or evening clear.

cultureNotes: In many Spanish-speaking places, people use both 12-hour and 24-hour time. For casual plans, context usually makes morning or evening clear.

nativeSpeech.title: How Carlos would say it naturally
notes: Native speakers often say ??A qu? hora?? for ?At what time??
?Nos vemos a las ocho? is a natural way to say ?See you at eight.?

commonMistakes.explanation: Only one o?clock uses es la.
Use a las to mean at a time.

commonMistakes.correction: ma?ana can mean tomorrow or morning.
commonMistakes.explanation: Context tells you which meaning is intended.

realLifeMission.title: Real Life Mission
realLifeMission.mission: Tell Carlos what time you practice Spanish today and make one plan using a day, a number, and a time.
successCriteria: Use at least three lesson vocabulary items.
Answer one follow-up question from Carlos.

review.summary: You practiced numbers & time for everyday Spanish.
canDo: Answer Carlos with simple complete sentences.
Use the Real Life Mission in a practical situation.
